plan_metadata.name: Feature Arm Deoploy Support Json Format And Multiple Templates

steps.description: Click the "Teams Agents and Apps" option in the "New Project" dropdown menu within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "General Teams Agent" option in the dropdown menu of the "Teams Agent or App Using Microsoft Teams SDK" selection dialog within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.

Click the "Tab" option in the dropdown menu under the "Teams Capability" selector within Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit in Visual Studio Code, where "A simple implementation of a web app that's ready to customize" is shown as the option description.
Click the "Default folder" input field in the "Workspace Folder" selection dialog at the top of the Visual Studio Code window to set the project root folder location.

@code open file #text:/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/${{var:app_name}}/m365agents.yml, in line 40 copy #text
```
        - path: ./infra/azure.json
          parameters: ./infra/azure.parameters.test.json
          deploymentName: test-json-format 
```
 then save this file.
Trigger the Print dialog in Visual Studio Code by pressing **Ctrl+P** from the main workspace, with the "MICROSOFT 365 AGENTS TOOLKIT" panel open on the left sidebar.
Type '.env.dev' into the Visual Studio Code search bar at the top of the interface to locate a recently opened environment configuration file.
Press the Enter key in the Visual Studio Code quick open input box to open the recently opened file ".env.dev".
